title: hieracium pilosella subsp. leucosphaericum
intro: Hieracium pilosella subsp. leucosphaericum, also known as mouse-ear hawkweed, is a subspecies of the perennial plant Hieracium pilosella. It is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the dandelion family and has been used in traditional medicine for its various properties. The plant contains flavonoids and sesquiterpene lactones, which have been found to have medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and have been used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Mouse-ear hawkweed can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in herbal remedies, such as salves and ointments, to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
